title: Cota melanoloma
intro: Cota melanoloma, also known as Cota or mountain everlasting, is a perennial plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cota melanoloma include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Cota melanoloma is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments, to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.

This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its flower structure and leaf arrangement, which are typical of the Asteraceae family. The genus Cota is a group of plants within this family that are native to the Mediterranean region. The specific species Cota melanoloma is further classified as a member of the Cota genus, which consists of several other related species.
